author: nflaig
content:
Hey all, was curious if anyone implemented a PoC already that can run on kurtosis, I only remember that Prysm did but not sure what's the status there.

been working on implementing this in Lodestar  based on what's in https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4484 and wanna check if there is another client to interop with

currently custom fork `eip7782` which is on top of fulu but this should be easy to rebase on gloas
```yaml
participants:
  - el_type: geth
    cl_type: lodestar
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:eip7782
    count: 2

network_params:
  eip7782_fork_epoch: 1

additional_services:
  - dora

dora_params:
  image: "nflaig/dora:eip7782-support"
```
needs custom dora image and `github.com/nflaig/ethereum-package@eip7782` to support all params and make dora behave properly

author: manunlp
content: cc <@399309815517544451> who implemented the PoC in Prysm

author: milos88
content:
I worked on PoC setup for Kurtosis couple months ago. Here is detailed writing: https://notes.ethereum.org/@miloss/Prototyping_EIP-7782

I worked on Geth, Dora, and Kurtosis changes, while <@845691100072509460> worked on Prysm (I believe there was some overlap with <@399309815517544451>'s work). This was done on top of Fulu, so maybe some rebasing would be required.

author: nflaig
content: ah great, wasn't aware that this existed, dora, eth pakage changes etc. are likely same tweaks I did, you also used `eip7782_fork_epoch` separate fork so in theory should be able to interop

author: nflaig
content: I did give this a try but it looks like Prysm doesn't actually upgrade the state at the fork transition so the state fork version doesn't match with Lodestar and they disconnect each other. I am also not sure if Prysm implemented all other changes from https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4484 like adjusting the rewards which would cause issues too. I guess for now it's probably best to wait for the spec to settle on this, and likely by then we wanna test it on top of gloas already.

author: milos88
content:
<@845691100072509460> can provide more details on prysm changes
but yes, it would probably be the best to rebase on top of gloas when specs are finalized
